LANDSCAPING EXPERTS

Landscaping Tailored to Portland’s Unique Climate

Expert care for gardens thriving in Oregon's rainy winters and dry summers. From moss-resistant designs to earthquake-resilient hardscaping, we solve local challenges.

What We Do

Our Services

Rain Garden Installation

Historic Property Landscaping

Earthquake-Resilient Hardscaping
SEE ALL SERVICES →

Who We Are

Rooted in Portland’s Landscape

Founded in 2012, Horizon Landscaping began with restoring historic Craftsman bungalows in the Hawthorne district. We specialize in solutions for damp Pacific Northwest conditions.

Our team combines horticultural knowledge with structural engineering for landscapes that endure. We prioritize native plants, proper drainage, and materials resistant to wood rot.

As active members of the Portland Urban Forestry Commission, we’re committed to sustainable practices that enhance our neighborhoods.

13+ Years
850+ Projects
98% Satisfaction
GET IN TOUCH →

Our Difference

Why Choose Horizon Landscaping

Moss Management

Specialized treatments to prevent slippery surfaces while preserving beneficial moss in shaded areas

Earthquake-Ready Designs

Retaining walls and hardscapes built with seismic movement in mind

Native Plant Expertise

Drought-resistant summer plantings that thrive in our wet winters

Winterization Plans

Protecting delicate plants from rare ice storms without harsh chemicals

Let’s Create a Landscape That Grows With You

Schedule a free site assessment to discuss solutions for your specific microclimate

Get a Free Estimate Call 555-lan-rq8

Client Feedback

What Our Clients Say

“

Horizon restored our 1915 bungalow's original garden using archived photos from the Historical Society. They found century-old rose cultivars at a specialty nursery and replicated the gravel walkways with perfect mortar consistency. The drainage system they discreetly installed saved our basement during last winter's record rainfall.

Mikael R. Historic Property Landscaping
“

Our yard in the West Hills used to become a swamp every winter. Horizon's terraced rain garden handles the runoff beautifully while attracting hummingbirds with red-flowering currants. Three years later, the plants have filled in exactly as promised - zero maintenance beyond seasonal pruning.

Sophia K. Rain Garden Installation
“

The gabion wall Horizon built along our slope in the Pearl hasn't budged through three minor quakes. They incorporated our existing concrete rubble into the design, saving hauling fees. The steel cages are planted with sedums that change color seasonally - functional and beautiful.

Carlos T. Earthquake-Resilient Hardscaping
“

After years of struggling with brown lawns in summer, we converted to a native meadow in our Belmont backyard. Horizon's plant selection has something blooming from March to October. Our kids love spotting the swallowtail butterflies that now visit daily.

Priya S. Native Meadow Conversion
“

Our narrow lot in Alberta Arts couldn't fit a traditional garden. Horizon's living wall produces enough lettuce and herbs for our family of four, plus flowers for cutting. The automatic irrigation saved our plants during last summer's heat dome when we were away.

James L. Vertical Garden Systems
“

Finding someone who understood the specific needs of our 1927 Tudor was challenging until we met Horizon. They replicated the original parterre garden using antique boxwood varieties and even matched the decomposed granite paths found in period photographs. The Historical Landmarks Commission approved their plans without revisions.

Naomi W. Historic Property Landscaping

Our Portfolio

Our Recent Work

Common Questions

Frequently Asked Questions

Will rain gardens breed mosquitoes?
Properly designed rain gardens drain within 48 hours, preventing mosquito breeding. We include mosquito-dunk tablets in overflow areas for added protection during wet springs.
How do they fare in drought?
Deep-rooted natives access water reserves other plants can't. During the 2022 drought, our rain gardens outperformed traditional lawns by 3:1 survival rates.
